topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone screen, a jarring splash of blue against the muted background of my afternoon. My breath hitched. I stared at the name: Mark. Mark, the architect of my awkward middle school years, the one whose shadow I’d spent a decade trying to outrun. A friend request. A deliberate, calculated move. My jaw tightened so hard I thought my molars might crack. I scrolled through my contacts, a frantic, jerky motion. Why now? What was he angling for? A cold knot formed in my stomach, a slow, heavy pressure spreading through my chest. I didn't tap 'accept.' I just let the screen sit there, a silent accusation. I threw my phone onto the couch cushion, the thud echoing too loudly in the quiet room.

[story 2]

When the alert chimed, Sarah nearly dropped her coffee mug. It was Mark. The boy who used to orchestrate elaborate, cruel pranks in the hallways, the one whose laughter always sounded like gravel grinding together. Sarah felt a flush creep up her neck, warm and undeniable. Her hands, gripping the mug handle, began to tremble slightly. She looked at the screen, her eyes narrowing, a sharp, defensive glint flashing in their depths. *This isn't funny,* she thought, the words tasting like ash. She didn't reply. Instead, she stood up abruptly, pacing the length of her small apartment, her movements jerky and agitated. Every muscle in her body felt coiled, ready to spring, a tight wire humming under her skin.

[story 3]

He had managed to breach the digital fortress I’d meticulously built around my personal space. A friend request from Mark. A slow burn started in my chest, a deep, simmering heat that radiated outwards. I picked up my phone, my fingers stiff, hovering over the message. A wave of pure irritation washed over me, so potent it made my vision swim for a second. I felt a distinct urge to slam the device down onto the nearest surface. I took a deep, shuddering breath, forcing the air into my lungs, trying to steady the rapid pulse hammering against my ribs. I deleted the notification immediately, a swift, decisive action meant to erase the intrusion.
